title: analytics — Eloquent observers
description: Four observers — one per owned entity. The event + provider-config + identity observers handle the load-bearing validation + snapshot + gate work; the delivery observer handles the delivered_provider_count aggregation on parent events.

handles: creating — snapshot attribution via AttributionContext (frozen jsonb into attribution column)

creating — snapshot consent via ConsentGate::has(subject, 'analytics') into consent_snapshot column; refuse creation when analytics consent missing AND category NOT IN ('essential-funnel-steps whitelist') — fires AnalyticsEventSuppressedByConsent instead of persisting
creating — validate properties against PropertyValidator (max 100 keys, max 1KB total, PII regex scan). Refuse with NOTIFICATIONS_ANALYTICS_PII_DETECTED on violation

creating — compute eligible_provider_count via AnalyticsProviderConfig fan-out with sampling roll; when zero, set status='suppressed_by_provider_off' and fire AnalyticsEventSuppressedBySampling / _SuppressedByConsent (whichever branch caused it)
creating — apply per-event-name category rules (funnel_step must carry properties.funnel_key + properties.step_key; experiment must carry properties.experiment_key + properties.variant)
created — fire AnalyticsEventCaptured
created — dispatch DispatchAnalyticsEventJob when eligible_provider_count > 0
created — fire AnalyticsFunnelStepReached when category='funnel_step'
created — fire AnalyticsExperimentAssigned when category='experiment' AND event_name='experiment_variant_assigned'

updating — refuse most updates. Only the state columns (status, delivered_provider_count) may be mutated; the immutable columns (properties, context, attribution, consent_snapshot, event_time) are frozen. Refusal writes an audit warning.
creating — refuse when tenant has no `analytics_provider_slot` entitlement remaining
creating — validate provider-specific config shape against schemas/providers/{provider}.provider.json
creating — validate enabled_event_types patterns (each must be a valid event-name pattern)
creating — validate sampling_rate in [0..1] AND for provider not in {posthog, custom_webhook}, refuse sampling_rate < 1.0 unless tenant has `analytics_sampling_config` entitlement
creating — validate batch_config against provider's max_batch_size + max_batch_bytes
creating — initialise circuit_breaker_state to closed
creating — seed retry_config from provider defaults if not caller-supplied
creating — when test_mode=true, dispatch DispatchToProviderJob with a synthetic test event to verify credentials + endpoint reachability
created — fire AnalyticsProviderConfigured
updating — refuse changes to `provider` column (immutable once created; delete + recreate to change providers)
updated — fire AnalyticsProviderConfigured with the list of changed columns
updated — when is_active flipped false, fire AnalyticsProviderDeactivated + drain the batch buffer for this config
deleting — drain the batch buffer for this config (flush any queued events) before soft-delete completes
deleted — fire AnalyticsProviderDeactivated with reason='soft_delete'
creating — enforce unique (tenant_id, anonymous_id) among non-deleted rows (Postgres unique index catches the race; this observer refuses proactively)
creating — set first_seen_at + last_seen_at to now() if not caller-set
creating — hash device_fingerprint if caller supplied a raw fingerprint (defensive; the endpoint should never accept raw)
created — fire AnalyticsIdentityCreated
updating — detect null → non-null transition on identified_user_id column

updated — on null → non-null identified_user_id transition: set identified_at=now(), fire AnalyticsIdentityIdentified, dispatch MergeAliasedEventsJob (when tenant has `analytics_identity_merge` entitlement)
updated — on non-null → null identified_user_id transition (deidentification): fire AnalyticsIdentitySplit
saving — update last_seen_at when any event references this identity (via touch())
creating — denormalise provider column from the referenced AnalyticsProviderConfig for query performance

updated — when status transitions TO 'delivered', increment parent AnalyticsEvent.delivered_provider_count atomically; when delivered_provider_count reaches eligible_provider_count, transition parent AnalyticsEvent.status → 'delivered' + fire AnalyticsEventDelivered

updated — when status transitions TO 'permanently_failed' AND all sibling deliveries are terminal AND some sibling delivered_provider_count < eligible_provider_count: transition parent AnalyticsEvent.status → 'partially_delivered' + fire AnalyticsEventPartiallyDelivered
updated — on any delivery error, invoke AnalyticsCircuitBreakerRegistry::recordFailure(provider_config_id) which may transition the circuit to open + fire AnalyticsProviderCircuitBreakerOpened
